open_questions: No disclosed plan from Steel Partners for use of the roughly $330 million net operating loss shell; base and bull scenarios are analyst assumptions, not filed guidance. Treat any position above a token watch weight as **[PENDING APPROVAL]**.

The FY2025 10-K and 2025–2026 10-Qs are not yet downloaded into `TPHS/investor-documents/sec-edgar/`; this report leans on unaudited IR press releases for 2025–2026 figures and should be reconciled against the audited 10-K once it is filed.
Confirm the OTC Pink closing price and shares outstanding against the next filed 10-Q.

Confirm that current and future buyers of TPHS common stock have no residual claim on the TPHGreenwich Trust (beneficial interests are stated to be non-transferable and limited to holders of record as of 2025-05-20).
Monitor for a Form 15 deregistration filing and any Schedule 13D/A amendments from Steel Partners affiliates.
thesis: Trinity Place Holdings Inc. is the legal successor to Syms Corp., the off-price retailer that liquidated more than a decade ago. **[Fact]** Until May 2025 it owned an indirect real estate joint venture in Greenwich, Connecticut and other legacy retail sites. **[Fact]** On May 20, 2025 the company transferred its 95 percent interest in that joint venture (a New Jersey property held through TPHGreenwich Holdings LLC) into a separate liquidating trust for the benefit of the people who held Trinity Place stock on that date. **[Fact]** Today Trinity Place has no operating business. **[Fact]** It owns a handful of dormant retail trademarks (FilenesBasement.com, the Stanley Blacker brand, Running of the Brides, and a slogan) and a very large tax loss carryforward, and it is controlled and financially supported by an affiliate of Steel Partners Holdings L.P. **[Fact]**
why_market_wrong: The stock trades on OTC Pink at about two cents a share and a market value near $1.5 million, priced like a dead shell. **[Fact]** The predictive attribute here is a **dormant asset** (Horizon Kinetics lens): the company's charter still protects roughly $330 million of federal and state net operating loss carryforwards against an ownership change, which only makes sense if the controlling holder, Steel Partners, sees future use for them. **[Inference]** No transaction is disclosed as of this writing, so this is a real option on Steel Partners' capital-allocation playbook, not a dated catalyst with a known date. **[Inference]**
